publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-04-20 23:59:06 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: English, Latin script.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Andrew Dickson White teaching collection

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: A teaching collection assembled by A.D. White about the medieval Church and the Lutheran Reformation. Contains 37 items, including four indulgences, the oldest being issued in 1259; a famous anti-Catholic print by the Lutheran artist Erhard Schön (1491-1542), titled "Klage Gottes über seinen Weinberg"; photographic image (printed on paper) titled, "Buddha as a Christian Saint at Palermo"; and the original porfolio case A.D. White used to carry materials to the classroom, which contains a list of items in his own handwriting. This material is a unique example of a small teaching collection with a selection of textual and visual materials ready for a class or a research seminar; it is an early example of how history was taught at Cornell in the 19th century. The archival documents themselves would be of interest to students and researchers in History, German studies, Religious Studies, as well as anyone interested in Cornell's early history.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Intellectual arrangement reflects A.D. White's original order. White's original handwritten list appears on the inside back cover of his original portfolio located in the last folder of the box. A facsimile copy of this list appears on the inside lid of the box. Physical arrangement grouped by size after processing and conservation treatment.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Andrew Dickson White teaching collection, #8928. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

did.unittitle: Letter ad executionem indulgentiarum

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From Heinrich Menger, expert in canon law (Kanoniker) from Zürich, commissioned by the Council of Basel (1346) to organize the dispensation of indulgences in the cities and dioceses of Constance, Bamberg, Würzburg, Augsburg, Eichstätt and Regensburg, in order to fund the council of reunification between the Western and Eastern Churches and to defray the costs of the delegation of the Greek Church and the Byzantine Emperor coming to Europe. Explains the procedure and appoints the Carthusian monk and abbot Johannes Rode to coordinate the project in, e.g., the vast diocese of Bamberg.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Transcription by Laurent Ferri, 2026, in the folder.

did.unittitle: Modern facsimile of letter ad executionem indulgentiarum
Papal bull issued by Pope Alexander IV

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Granting an indulgence of a hundred days to Christians who made offerings to a Cistercian convent in Worms. Agnani.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Transcription by Laurent Ferri, 2026, in the folder.

did.unittitle: Letter from the prince-archbishop and Elector of Trier/Treves, Johann II of Baden

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Authorizing the sale of indulgences to fund the transformation of the church of the Blessed Mary in Wolf into a "collegiatam ecclesiam cum numero sacerdotum et clericorum ac cornrnuni mensa et dormitorio."

did.unittitle: Letter of indulgence ad opus sancte cruciate (to fund the crusade against the Turks) or indulgence certificate (Ablass)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed in Augsburg in 1482 and issued by the Franciscan priest Petrus Cristannus, Subkommissar für den Türkenablass in Bavaria. Facsimile. The (printed) original document is kept Rare Books BX2282 A2 1482 Broadside box + The letter of indulgence was printed many times, with almost identical wording, in the years 1482 and 1483, mostly on behalf of these individual commissaries. Outside the Vatican, only seven copies of 1482/3 indulgences are preserved today: one is kept in Freiburg; one in the Bayerische Staatsbibliothek; one at the Bodleian (Oxford); one at the University of Pennsylvania; one in the Huntington Library; one in private hands; and one at Cornell.

originalsloc.head: Existence and Location of Originals
originalsloc.p: Indulgentia : [1 July 1480], Rare Books BX2282 .A2 1482 Broadside box +. Rare and Manuscript Collections.

did.unittitle: Document concerning the noble collegiate Church

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Of the 11,000 Holy Virgins (St. Ursula) in Cologne and its management by an abbess (abbatissa totusque conventus ecclesie sanctarum undecim milium virginum in Colona).

did.unittitle: Indulgence printed for sale to the visitors to the scared shrine in the cathedral of Treves
Ordinance regarding the sale of indulgences with the inclusion of "confessional letters"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Issued by papal delegates in the dioces of Treves/Trier.

did.unittitle: Ordinance regarding the sale of indulgences benefiting the Carmelites of Heilbronn

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Granted by Pope Alexander VI.

did.unittitle: 19th century color print showing the Shrine of Mary in Aachen Cathedral is a reliquary

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Donated on the order of the chapter of Mary around 1220 and consecrated in 1239.

did.unittitle: 17th or 18th century engraving showing the Holy Tunic of Argenteuil

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Purported to be the garment which Jesus Christ wore during his Passion. The relic was given in the 9th century by Emperor Charlemagne to his daughter, who was the abbess of the Argenteuil monastery.

did.unittitle: Illustrated broadsheet from the engraving shop of the "Heiligentrucker" Gerhard Altzenbach (1590-1672) in Cologne

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With a map of the city of Trier/ Treves surrounded by images of the relics kept in the cathedral of Treves, including the Holy Tunic; the head reliquary of Saint Helena; the reliquary shrine of Saint Maternus; the reliquary shrine of Saint Blaise and other saints; the Holy Nail, said to be a nail of the True Cross, given by Saint Helena to the church in Trier according to the tradition; or the silver arm reliquary of Saint Anne, made in the 15th century.

did.unittitle: Illustrated broadsheet printed in Aix-la-Chapelle/Aachen

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Occupied by the French at the time; illustration showing the relics kept in the cathedral.

did.unittitle: Devotional image showing a holy relic kept in Aachen/Aix-la-Chapelle

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Namely the Holy Spear, alleged to be the lance that pierced the side of Jesus Christ as he hung on the Cross

did.unittitle: Two devotional images showing the Holy Nail

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Relic kept in Aachen/Aix-la-Chapelle

did.unittitle: Attestation of the authenticity of relics kept in Aachen/Aix-la-Chapelle

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: By the dean (decanus) of the Saint-Paul Basilica in Trier

did.unittitle: Attestation of relics

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Authenticity certificate regarding the holy remains of the Spanish priest Padre Beato Giuseppe Calasanzio della Madre di Dio (1557-1648), fondatore dell'Ordine dei Padri Scolopi, issued on March 8, 1755, by Silvestro Merani, titular Bishop of Porphyreon (1688-1764).

did.unittitle: Document regarding the housing of the remains of Joseph Calasanz /Giuseppe Calasanzio

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Under the main altar of the Church of San Pantaleo in Rome

did.unittitle: Attestation of relics, Rome
Attestation of relics, Rome
Two prayer sheets from a Jesuit pocket devotional

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With prayers against the demons, issued by the Inquisition in Turin. Oratio Contra Omnes, tum Maleficorum, tum Daemonum Incursus is a prayer against those who use the art of the devils. It bears two printed stamps of the Jesuit order. There is also another folded sheet, with portraits of the Saints surrounding a Cross with the Virgin Mary and prayers contra mala pestem et…tempestates.

did.unittitle: Document about Johann Wilhelm Schmitz (1774-1841)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Kapitularvikar for the diocese of Cologne.

did.unittitle: Editto riguardo ai comportamenti perseguibili dal Sant'Uffizio all'interno della diocesi cremonese

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Document established under the authority of the Inquisitor Silvestro Martini, listing all the heretical behaviors punishable by the Holy Office within the Diocese of Cremona (Italy), including witchcraft, as well as the printing or distribution of books written by Jews or books of theology not reviewed and approved by the bishop.

did.unittitle: Illustrated broadsheet (Protestant propaganda) showing King Gustav Adolf of Sweden defeating the Catholic beast in Augsburg
Erhard Schön (1491-1542), "Klage Gottes über seinen Weinberg"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Similar engraving kept at the British Library, but with 140 lines of verse by Hans Sachs. This is satire of the decadent and corrupt Church of Rome, by a Lutheran artist. Three clerics are trying to escape a fenced-in vineyard guarded by a huge dog. Behind them, the Pope as well as monks are uprooting and burning stems and use the blood of Christ (who appears crucified on a vine) to grow a dead tree. Papal indulgences and other symbols of the alleged corruption of the Roman Catholic Church hand from the branches of these trees. By contrast, on the left, a devout congregation is listening to a Protestant preacher who celebrates a culture of life.

did.unittitle: Map of the city of Treves (with its many churches)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From Civitates orbis terrarum, a collection of maps engraved by Franz Hogenberg (1539-1590).

did.unittitle: Documents regarding Die mystische Vermählung der hl. Katharina
Article from the Norwegian newspaper Aftenposten reporting a case of witchcraft
Papal brief issued by Pope Paul V

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: regarding the beatification of Giovacchino Piccolomini (1268-1305), whose vita contained many accounts of alleged miracles and cure at this prayerful intercession.

did.unittitle: Papal brief issued by Pope Urbain VIII, regarding miracles
Printed photograph showing the statue of Saint Josaphat in Palermo

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Acquired by A.D. White in 1901 and described by him as "Buddha as a Christian Saint" (autographed note kept in the folder, plus notes added by Laurent Ferri, 2026).

did.unittitle: A.D. White's original portfolio used to carry material to lectures

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains original handwritten item list.
